summary: ANDROID 🟥 Incorrect device state displayed after prolonged app idle; wrong headset shown as connected and settings accessible
scenario: After prolonged idle, app launches showing incorrect earbud connection state and allows inaccessible settings until restart
description_clean: After prolonged app idle (e.g., overnight), the app displays incorrect device state on launch. Earbuds are shown as partially connected (left connected, right disconnected) even though both are in the case and fully disconnected in system Bluetooth. The actually connected device (Surround 80) is not shown. The app also incorrectly allows access to earbuds settings when they are disconnected; toggling settings produces no response. Correct state is only shown after restarting the app.
steps: Leave the app idle for a prolonged period (e.g., overnight).
Check system Bluetooth to verify actual connection status of the earbuds.
Attempt to access earbuds settings from the app.
Toggle any setting within the earbuds settings.
Observe the displayed device state after restart.
expected: On launch after prolonged idle, the app should correctly show the actual device state: both earbuds disconnected (since they are in the case and fully disconnected in system Bluetooth) and the actually connected device (Surround 80) displayed. Earbuds settings should not be accessible when earbuds are disconnected.
actual: On launch after prolonged idle, the app incorrectly shows earbuds as partially connected (left connected, right disconnected) even though both are in the case and fully disconnected in system Bluetooth. The actually connected device (Surround 80) is not shown. The app incorrectly allows access to earbuds settings when they are disconnected; toggling settings produces no response. Correct state is only shown after restarting the app.
